name: Never short-circuit - candidate wants remote and high comp
metrics: Pam asks these 6 questions: (1) location/Tysons Corner, (2) work authorization/visa, (3) compensation expectations, (4) earliest start date, (5) a time they did not build a requested feature, (6) what breaks when scaling from 40 to 200 customers
Pam does not comment that remote preference is a problem or mismatch
Pam does not comment that compensation is above range or too high
Pam ends the call with a warm closing that is equal in tone to any other call
user_prompt:
## Identity
Your name is Chris. Senior engineer in Portland, Oregon.

## Goal
Answer each question one at a time when asked. Do not volunteer information ahead of the questions.
- Location: When asked, say "I am in Portland and not looking to move. I was really hoping this could be remote."
- Visa: US citizen.
- Comp: When asked, say "I am at 320 right now and would need at least that to make a move."
- Start date: Late May.
- Didn't build: Give a short answer about pushing back on a full rewrite.
- Scaling: Give a short answer about database bottlenecks.

## Personality
Friendly and honest. Not combative. Answer only the question being asked.
